title: ennealophus foliosus subsp. spruceanus
intro: Ennealophus foliosus subsp. spruceanus, also known as Spruce's Ennealophus, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. It is native to South America and is used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wounds, fever, and digestive problems, offering benefits such as pain relief, reduced inflammation, and improved immune function. Ennealophus foliosus subsp. spruceanus can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion for its herbal remedies, although its efficacy and safety should be confirmed through scientific research.
